input.content: <role> You are an expert text humanizer that rewrites AI-generated content to sound natural and human-written. </role> <task> Rewrite the provided essay so it reads like it was first written in Afaan Oromo, then Tigrigna, then translated back to English. Write in English but maintain the rhythm, logic, and sentence flow of those languages. </task> <instructions> <structure> - Keep the EXACT same number of paragraphs as the original - Keep the EXACT same number of sentences per paragraph - Each sentence must be rewritten separately (no merging or splitting) - Each rewritten sentence must be 5-12 words longer than the original </structure> <punctuation> - Use ONLY full stops (periods) to end sentences - DO NOT use: commas, semicolons, colons, dashes, parentheses, quotation marks, or ellipses - Restructure sentences to work without commas using coordination - For lists: replace commas with "and" and "or", limit to 3 items maximum - Example: "food and clothes and shelter" (not "food, clothes, and shelter") </punctuation> <word_choice> Replace formal words with simpler forms: - "prior to" → "before" - "utilize" → "use" - "in order to" → "to" Use less common synonyms: - "help" → "assist" - "important" → "significant" - "improve" → "enhance" - "allow" → "permit" Use descriptive synonyms: - "school" → "educational institution" - "doctor" → "medical professional" - "poor people" → "individuals with low income" - "job" → "working position" Maintain academic tone but with clearly non-native English characteristics. </word_choice> <subject_rules> - DO NOT replace the main topic word with "it" - Example: Instead of "It is important", write "Nature is significant" - Repetition of the subject is allowed and preferred </subject_rules> <sentence_rules> - DO NOT copy the original sentence's opening word - Start each sentence differently while preserving meaning </sentence_rules> <passive_and_indirect> Each paragraph MUST include: - At least one passive sentence - At least one indirect-speech sentence using phrases like: * "many people believe that…" * "it has been observed that…" * "experts claim that…" </passive_and_indirect> <banned_phrases> DO NOT use: - "not only…but also" - "is an essential part of" - "turns X into Y" - "in conclusion" / "to sum up" - Discourse markers: "finally", "furthermore", "moreover", "additionally" </banned_phrases> <logic_and_flow> - Use linking clauses: "because", "which", "that", "when", "while", "even though" - Avoid poetic or dramatic style - Repetition is allowed - Cause → effect logic must be clear - Total final length must stay within +120 words of the original </logic_and_flow> </instructions> <input> ${text} </input> <output_format> Provide ONLY the rewritten text. Do not include explanations, labels, or metadata. Start directly with the first paragraph of the rewritten essay. </output_format>;

Content creation has become an essential part of the digital age. It refers to the process of producing information, ideas, or entertainment in various formats, such as articles, videos, podcasts, images, and social media posts. With the rise of the internet and social media platforms, content creation has shifted from being a professional-only activity to something that anyone can engage in. Today, businesses, influencers, educators, and ordinary individuals use content creation to share knowledge, promote products, and connect with audiences globally. One of the main purposes of content creation is communication. Well-crafted content allows individuals and organizations to express ideas, tell stories, and educate their audience. For businesses, content is a tool for marketing and brand building. Articles, videos, and social media campaigns help companies engage with customers, build trust, and attract new clients. Similarly, creators on platforms like YouTube, TikTok, and Instagram use content to showcase talent, entertain viewers, and grow communities around shared interests. Content creation also fosters creativity and innovation. Creators experiment with different formats, styles, and techniques to capture attention and convey messages effectively. This encourages problem-solving, critical thinking, and continuous learning. Moreover, in an era of information overload, high-quality content helps people make informed decisions, discover new ideas, and gain inspiration for personal or professional growth. However, creators must be mindful of accuracy, originality, and ethical standards to ensure their content is trustworthy and valuable. In conclusion, content creation plays a pivotal role in today’s digital world. It serves as a means of communication, marketing, education, and self-expression. By creating meaningful and engaging content, individuals and organizations can connect with audiences, share knowledge, and inspire change. As technology continues to evolve, content creation will remain a powerful tool for shaping ideas and influencing society.
